items.title: Ageing 'Nashos' call for priority as all veterans get equal access to health benefits

children.content: All Australian veterans will soon have access to claim compensation for health issues, fulfilling a royal commission recommendation, but those conscripted during the Vietnam War say their advanced age means their claims should receive priority.

items.title: Housing shortage does not deter town from embracing migrants

children.content: The township of Naracoorte, nestled in South Australia's rural heartland, has put out the welcome mat for migrants, who have helped enrich its social fabric and boost the economy.

items.title: 'Worst' wind lull pushes green poster child South Australia to its power limits

children.content: South Australia is at the bleeding edge of the energy transition but record low winds have pushed its grid to the brink of its limits.

items.title: They didn't know what footy was doing to their boy. 'Now it's too late'

children.content: A talent and a team player, Nick Lowden excelled at Aussie rules. But the knocks, bumps, hits and collisions were adding up.

items.title: Volunteers say hotline told them to collect dead bird against government advice

children.content: South Australian citizen scientists say they called the Emergency Animal Disease Hotline to report a dead sea bird and were given advice that contradicts official government messaging.

items.title: The dry heart of Australia is now awash with water

children.content: The myth of an inland sea lured 19th century European explorers into the heart of Australia. But where they found only desert, this year it's awash, with water levels at Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre now the best some locals have ever seen.

articles.title: Heavy rain to soak Australia's east as un-El-Niño-like weather continues

children.content: For an El Niño year, 2026 is so far the wettest on record for Australia, and an influx of tropical moisture is about to deliver another round of widespread rain.

items.title: Report finds 'extensive' coal and gas presence in schools and children's sport

children.content: Santos, BHP, Woodside and others have an "extensive presence" in childhood settings through more than 260 programs, a new report identifies, as senators say classroom programs and lessons written by the industry should be examined through a senate inquiry.

items.title: Australia and Vanuatu set to sign strategic pact after months of negotiations

children.content: Vanuatu's Prime Minister Jotham Napat and Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese are set to ink the Nakamal Agreement, almost 10 months after Mr Napat pulled out of a planned signing ceremony in Port Vila, citing concerns around sovereignty.

items.title: Real estate has been an easy target for criminals laundering money, until now

children.content: Anti-money laundering laws mean that for the first time, real estate agents, lawyers, accountants and others must report suspicious transactions.

articles.title: Major capital cities record worst housing auction clearance rates in years

children.content: Sydney and Melbourne record their weakest auction results in years, with fewer than half of all homes going under the hammer finding a buyer.

articles.title: Treasurer promises to 'fix' property tax oversight, but won't say how exactly

items.title: NT Police says public has no need to be concerned about new officer class

children.content: The Northern Territory Police Force has moved to reassure Territorians that new, armed public safety officers due to hit the beat from today are fully trained to carry guns.

items.title: Telstra phasing out CAN Radio phone system amid customer safety concerns

children.content: Telstra's Customer Access Network Radio has been a lifeline for many for more than 40 years but its technology has reached the end of its life. Customers now fear a new system will not be as reliable in an emergency.

items.title: Death of 'well-respected' doctor leads push to review fatigue policy

children.content: Doctors are calling for Tasmania's Department of Health to examine its fatigue management policies after a doctor died on the state's roads. Artyom Avetisyan, a cardiothoracic surgery registrar at the Royal Hobart Hospital, has been remembered by hospital staff as a kind and compassionate colleague who will be "greatly missed".
